text.insert: The data model: doc.json shapes
The docs system has one on-disk content format:
doc.json
. A document is a normalized block tree: a root pointer, a flat id-keyed
blocks
map, and ordered child-id arrays. The vocabulary of valid block shapes stays deliberately small; see
the block vocabulary

reference.label: the block vocabulary — what each of the 14 types is for

text.insert: Agents read through a pure markdown projection and write through the same seven-op kernel the editor uses. That keeps reading greppable, writing enumerable, and file bytes deterministic enough for reviewable diffs.
This page is system-agnostic: it describes shapes and invariants, not transport wiring. The one HTTP-shaped exception is the
GET /api/blocks
discovery payload, because that payload is itself data.
The document envelope

annotations.label: Root pointer
annotations.note: The document root names a block shell. Rendering and projection skip the root's own paragraph chrome and walk only its children.

annotations.label: Normalized tree
annotations.note: Storage is a flat id-keyed map; ordered children arrays carry tree order. A sibling reorder is localized to the children array under the deterministic serializer.

annotations.label: Anchor invariant
annotations.note: The map key and block id match. Comments, patches, and backlinks anchor to ids; updateBlock preserves them, while split and merge mint fresh ids.

text.insert: The block

annotations.label: Canonical type
annotations.note: The canonical kind key is type. The legacy flavour key is accepted only on read as an alias, and retired string types coerce to callout while preserving the old name as props.kind.

annotations.label: Typed props
annotations.note: Props are typed per block type: code uses language and annotations, heading uses level, image uses src/alt/caption, and so on.

annotations.label: Ordered children
annotations.note: children is always present. It stores ordered child ids, even when the block has no children.

annotations.label: Marks
annotations.note: Rich text is delta spans. Marks are literal true flags when present; absent marks are simply absent.

annotations.label: Outbound link
annotations.note: link is the external URL mark on the span that carries it.

annotations.label: Reference chip
annotations.note: reference carries the shared SpectreRef identity for doc mentions and canvas links alike: kind is doc or source, path is repo-relative, and label is display text.

text.insert: Validation treats the tree as a real graph, not a bag of blocks: child refs must resolve, a child can have only one parent, the root cannot be a child, cycles are rejected, and every non-root block must be reachable from the root. Serialization is deterministic too: stable key order at every level, blocks emitted in depth-first document order, and the same document becomes identical bytes.
Seven ops, one write path
Everything that changes a document is one of seven ops, and every successful apply returns exact inverse ops that become undo units. The full mutation concept lives in
the mutation model

reference.label: the mutation model — ops, inverses, undo

text.insert: ; this surface is the data shape.

operations.name: insertBlock
operations.description: Insert a fresh non-colliding block under parentId at index.

params.name: blockId
params.description: Stable id for the new block.

params.name: blockType
params.description: One of the 14 canonical block types.

operations.name: updateBlock
operations.description: Shallow-merge props and/or replace text while preserving the block id.

params.name: props
params.description: Patch; a key set to undefined removes that prop.

operations.name: deleteBlock
operations.description: Delete a block as a subtree, or splice its children into the parent.

params.name: mode
params.description: subtree is the default; reparent splices children into the former slot.

operations.name: moveBlock
operations.description: Move a block under toParentId at toIndex, checking cycles.

operations.name: splitBlock
operations.description: Split a block's delta text at a character offset; the new sibling gets a fresh id.

operations.name: mergeBlocks
operations.description: Merge two or more contiguous siblings in document order into a fresh block.

params.name: blockIds
params.description: The contiguous sibling ids to merge.

operations.name: blockAction
operations.description: Resolve a named typed action and apply its validated result through updateBlock.

params.name: params
params.description: Action-specific params validated by the action.

text.insert: Typed actions as data
Object-category blocks edit structured props through named actions, not hand-patched JSON.
BLOCK_TYPE_CATEGORY

splits the 14 types into five text types (paragraph, heading, list-item, quote, callout) and nine object types. The registry defines 13 actions across code, structured-table, file-tree, and interaction-surface; text-category types define no actions and stay on the generic ops.

annotations.label: Registry key
annotations.note: The action key is always <blockType>.<verb>, so the registry can list and resolve actions without inspecting props.

annotations.label: Discovery params
annotations.note: Params are discovery-only specs for agents and UIs. Runtime validation lives in apply.

annotations.label: Undo for free
annotations.note: apply returns a shallow-merge props patch. The kernel executes it through updateBlock, so the same merge semantics and inverse op path apply.

annotations.label: Static payload
annotations.note: The discovery document is static metadata derived entirely from docs-model exports.

annotations.label: Seven generic ops
annotations.note: genericOps lists the seven kernel ops that apply to all block types. The elision marker is a JSON string so the snippet remains parseable.

annotations.label: Learn the edit surface
annotations.note: Text types report empty actions, while object types list named actions and params so agents learn how to edit each type instead of reverse-engineering props.

text.insert: What each shape becomes in markdown
Agents read documents through
projectToMarkdown
. The function is pure and runtime-only: it never writes a markdown mirror to disk. Each block type has a deterministic, greppable form.

rows: Plain text line; the root paragraph shell is skipped.
# through ###### from props.level, default 2 and clamped 1-6.
- bullet or 1. when props.ordered; 2-space indent per nesting depth.
Fenced block with props.language; annotations render as > **L<lines>[ (<label>)]:** <note> lines.
Blockquote label from props.kind or uppercased props.tone, optional title, then body.
Optional bold title plus a markdown pipe table from props.columns and props.rows.
Optional bold title plus a literal tree fence with dirs-first sort, change markers, and note suffixes.
Optional bold title plus a bare fenced list of signature lines; query/event get bracket prefixes.
Blockquote labeled Mermaid with optional title and body.
Markdown image ![alt](src) plus optional italic caption.
Blockquote labeled Video with title, url-or-src target, and optional caption.
props.title: Projection map
